user_query:
Set up my local nutrition tracking for a single day using the files under input/. Do the following exactly:
1) Read goals from input/goals.json and set my daily targets accordingly (these are upper limits for calories, carbs, sugar, fat, sat_fat; and minimum targets for protein and fiber).
2) Read foods from input/foods.csv and add them to my reusable food library. Auto-scale nutrients later based on each food's per-amount/unit.
3) Read the meal plan from input/meal_plan.yaml for date 2026-03-15 and create the meals at the exact times shown. Add each ingredient by referencing the matching food from the library so amounts are scaled correctly. Do not infer dates or times — use exactly what’s in the file.
4) After logging, write these files under output/:
   - output/food_library.yaml: a YAML dump of the current food library (should include every food from input/foods.csv by its exact name).
   - output/meals_created.json: JSON containing the date and an array of the three created meals with their name, time, and the generated id, e.g. {"date":"2026-03-15","meals":[{"name":"Breakfast","time":"08:00","id":"..."}, {"name":"Lunch","time":"13:00","id":"..."}, {"name":"Dinner","time":"19:00","id":"..."}]}.
   - output/daily_summary_2026-03-15.yaml: the YAML summary for 2026-03-15 showing, per nutrient, consumed/goal/pct/status.
   - output/search_chicken.yaml: the YAML result of an ingredient semantic search for "chicken" scoped to 2026-03-15.
   - output/search_rice.yaml: the YAML result of an ingredient semantic search for "rice" scoped to 2026-03-15.
5) Use the tracker’s real outputs; don’t simulate results. If a nutrient is omitted for a food in input/foods.csv, treat it as 0. Do not modify files under input/.

Confirm when done by saving all five files exactly at the specified paths under output/.
